title: Blotting Techniques & PCR
author: Garg, HK
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Blotting is a technique used to transfer and detect DNA, RNA, or proteins on a membrane, which allows researchers to identify specific sequences and analyze gene expression. Techniques such as Southern blotting for DNA, Northern blotting for RNA, and Western blotting for proteins serve as essential tools in genomics, diagnostics, and biotechnology. 
 On the other hand, PCR amplifies minute quantities of DNA, enabling scientists to create millions of copies of a specific sequence in just a few hours. It is highly useful in genetic testing, forensic analysis, and infectious disease detection.
steps_raw:
Blotting is a technique used to transfer and detect DNA, RNA, or proteins on a membrane, which allows researchers to identify specific sequences and analyze gene expression. Techniques such as Southern blotting for DNA, Northern blotting for RNA, and Western blotting for proteins serve as essential tools in genomics, diagnostics, and biotechnology. 
 On the other hand, PCR amplifies minute quantities of DNA, enabling scientists to create millions of copies of a specific sequence in just a few hours. It is highly useful in genetic testing, forensic analysis, and infectious disease detection.
